content: Just as second-language vocabulary acquisition of the word 'reflexion' requires repeated contextualized encounter and reflective rehearsal to move it from passive recognition to active use, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be re-designed to mimic this human incremental learning process. By framing LLM self-reflection as repeated contextual exposure to inconsistent reasoning perspectives (rather than one-off correction), we can build agents that retain generalized reflective reasoning skills instead of just fixing individual errors.
